Empires & Puzzles Amber Hero Review: The Vampire Blood Warrior

Amber hits hard, heals herself for 100% of the damage, and punishes anyone trying to heal her target. She's the ultimate 'Lone Ranger' of the Morlovia family. But do her stats hold up in the modern era? We dive into her kit to see if this vampire warrior still has bite.
Amber: The Self-Sustaining Sniper Who Hates Healers
Amber: The "Lone Ranger" Vampire Who Drinks Your Milkshake
Amber is a 5-star Legendary Fire hero from the

(Halloween) event. Released in late 2023, she arrived as a self-sufficient "Lone Ranger" type—a Fighter class hero designed to take care of herself while punishing enemies. She doesn't have a costume yet, so this review focuses entirely on her base form. While she was once a terrifying sniper, the sands of time (and power creep) have shifted around her. Is she still a bloodthirsty warrior, or just a dusty relic in the crypt?

Amber's design screams "Vampire General." She sports a slicked-back, Bride-of-Frankenstein-meets-Dracula hairstyle and armor that looks like it was forged in a blood bank. She wields a massive spear and seems to be levitating a globule of blood, which is... sanitary. It's a striking, aggressive look that fits her "I don't need a healer, I
Here is where the age spots start to show. With
, Amber is significantly behind the modern curve.
Respectable for her era, but modern snipers are pushing 1300+ raw attack.
These are the danger zones. Modern heroes boast 1300+ Defense and 2300+ Health. Amber is squishy by today's standards, meaning her self-heal mechanic is absolutely critical for her survival.

A modern sniper with 1300 Attack and 500% damage hits for ~6500. Amber's high percentage keeps her damage relevant, even if her stats are lower.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family and her passives do a lot of heavy lifting to keep her viable.
provides mana generation (+3% to +9%). Unlike many families that require 2+ heroes, this bonus starts at
. This innate +3% makes tuning her mana speed much easier.

. If she fires and the enemy has a riposte, taunt, or defense up, she might just take it for herself before hitting. If there are no buffs (or the 35% fail case), she deals extra damage to everyone. It's a win-win passive that adds utility or extra chip damage.
is the perfect Aether Power for her. It heals her for a percentage of normal damage dealt. Since she is a Fighter class and wants to stay alive to snipe again, any extra trickle healing is welcome.

Upon release, Amber was a "Lone Ranger" powerhouse. Players loved her ability to sustain herself without a healer. She was often compared to a "Red Sekhmet" but single-target, or a blend of the Lunar New Year heroes.
The stat gap is real. Her 1689 HP is paper-thin compared to the 2500+ HP behemoths released recently. She dies much faster now, often before she can fire her self-healing nuke.
. She is no longer a top-tier defense hero. However, she remains
for mid-game players or in specific wars (like Rush Attack) where her average speed is mitigated, or against heavy healer teams where her healing block disrupts the enemy flow.
As of this review, Amber does not have a costume.
Amber's entire kit revolves around dealing damage to heal herself. Therefore, you must prioritize Attack to maximize both her kill pressure and her survivability.
Maximizes the 605% hit. Higher damage = Higher self-heal.
She remains somewhat squishy until she fires.
Keeps her alive longer to hopefully fire.
Reduces her snipe damage and, ironically, reduces her healing capability.
is the only way to go. If she doesn't hit hard, she doesn't heal enough, and she doesn't kill.
100% heal on damage is incredible. She can reset her health bar.
The healing block + damage punishment is a nightmare for teams relying on heal-over-time or burst healers.
Her passive gives her utility beyond just damage.
605% is still a very high percentage.
Her defensive stats make her a glass cannon in the modern era.
In a meta dominated by hit-all or hit-3 monsters, a sniper—even a strong one—often feels too slow to clear the board.
If the 65% steal fails against a Reflector, she kills herself.
Hitting a minion reduces her damage, which directly reduces her healing.
Her Average speed becomes Very Fast. She can fire repeatedly, staying at full health and locking down enemies.
If she steals buffs, she becomes stronger.
Her self-sustain is excellent for long stages where you might not want to bring two healers.
Perfect for finishing off teams where a healer is the last one standing.
Amber works best when she can guarantee a kill or when she is protected until she fires.
. Dropping defense increases her damage, which massively increases her healing.
. High sustain red team. Boldtusk buffs attack (more healing for Amber), Grazul blocks ailments.
. Overwhelm the enemy with healing reduction and ailments.
Minions act as meat shields. If Amber hits a minion, she deals little damage and heals for almost nothing.
If she misses, she heals for zero.
A miss is fatal for her sustain loop.
If you pull Amber early, she is a
. Her ability to heal herself means she can often finish hard map stages alone when the rest of your team is dead. Max her out and emblem her for Attack.

She is likely a depth hero for you. Don't prioritize Alpha Aethers on her unless you simply love her playstyle or lack modern Red snipers. She can be useful in Tower events due to her self-heal, saving you from using items or healer slots.
The community immediately dubbed her a "Lone Ranger" hero due to her self-centered kit. One player noted,
"I like her, she hits hard but then heals like Sekhmet… maybe I’ll try to get this one."
There was debate about her vs. Xiaotu (Lunar New Year).
"She has 50% less dmg to Xiaotu... Xiaotu might be better?"
The consensus was that while Xiaotu hits 3 with passives, Amber's raw survival was her niche.
Players lamented the difficulty of pulling her, with one noting,
"She’s going to be almost impossible to get. Not only is she in the 0.3%, it is shared by 5 other heroes."

Amber is a vampire who brings her own dinner to the party. While she lacks the raw stats of the latest "Construct" or "Astral" heroes, her 605% hit and 100% vampiric heal keep her mechanically relevant. She is a fun, selfish survivor who punishes healers and refuses to die. If you can keep her alive long enough to fire once, she might just stay alive forever.
A solid B-tier hero in the modern meta, but an S-tier survivor in our hearts (and in map stages).
Is Amber still worth it in Empires & Puzzles? We analyze her stats, 'Spear of Blood Pact' skill, and current viability in this in-depth review.
